query_normalized: quantifying the complexity of knot diagrams via crossing number and braid index mathematics
rationale: This paper belongs in a literature review for knot complexity quantification because it measures knot complexity invariants (unknotting number, alteration number) in the same mathematical domain (knot theory), satisfying criterion (b) - it studies a related complexity variable on the user's domain even though it uses different specific measures (unknotting number vs. crossing number/braid index).
